tweets.text:
AI demand is fueling China’s economy:

Chinese semiconductor exports surged +111% YoY in May, to a record $36 billion.

This marks the largest annual increase in 13 years.

Outbound shipments of computers and parts jumped +66% YoY, to ~$27 billion, the 2nd-largest on record.

This surge was largely price-driven, with Chinese chip export volumes rising +2% over the same period, as global shortages caused by the AI data center buildout pushed semiconductor prices sharply higher.

Together, chips and computers accounted for roughly half of China's total export and import growth in May.

As a result, overall exports rose +19% YoY, while imports soared +27% YoY, leaving a trade surplus of $105.4 billion, the largest since January.

AI infrastructure spending is reshaping the Chinese economy.
tweets.full_text:
AI demand is fueling China’s economy:

Chinese semiconductor exports surged +111% YoY in May, to a record $36 billion.

This marks the largest annual increase in 13 years.

Outbound shipments of computers and parts jumped +66% YoY, to ~$27 billion, the 2nd-largest on record.

This surge was largely price-driven, with Chinese chip export volumes rising +2% over the same period, as global shortages caused by the AI data center buildout pushed semiconductor prices sharply higher.

Together, chips and computers accounted for roughly half of China's total export and import growth in May.

As a result, overall exports rose +19% YoY, while imports soared +27% YoY, leaving a trade surplus of $105.4 billion, the largest since January.

AI infrastructure spending is reshaping the Chinese economy.

tweets.text:
BREAKING: President Trump has signed a pair of Executive Orders aimed at speeding up the development of advanced quantum computers.

Details include:

1. Order 1 directs federal agencies to work with the private sector and academics to deploy a quantum computer powerful enough to conduct scientific research by 2028

2. Order 2 directs agencies and government security experts to prepare for quantum systems that can evade standard encryption more quickly than previously anticipated

3. The orders coincide with billions of dollars in funding for quantum companies being awarded by the Commerce Department

President Trump is ramping up support for quantum.
tweets.full_text:
BREAKING: President Trump has signed a pair of Executive Orders aimed at speeding up the development of advanced quantum computers.

Details include:

1. Order 1 directs federal agencies to work with the private sector and academics to deploy a quantum computer powerful enough to conduct scientific research by 2028

2. Order 2 directs agencies and government security experts to prepare for quantum systems that can evade standard encryption more quickly than previously anticipated

3. The orders coincide with billions of dollars in funding for quantum companies being awarded by the Commerce Department

President Trump is ramping up support for quantum.

tweets.text:
Markets in Taiwan and South Korea are experiencing unprecedented inflows:

Total equity fund inflows into Taiwan have risen to +155% of assets under management (AUM) since January 2024, the largest among all global markets.

South Korea has followed closely, at +150% of AUM over the same period, tripling so far in 2026.

Both are now running at least +500% above every other market.

South Korea also leads all markets with +45% of AUM in inflows year-to-date.

This is followed by Brazil, at +25%, and Taiwan at +18%.

Capital is flooding into Asia at an unprecedented pace.

tweets.text:
🇨🇳 #China Seeks to Unwind Car Purchase Limit, Boost Value Chain - Ministry of Commerce
- China aims to accelerate the elimination of unreasonable curbs on automobile consumption while expanding consumption across the industry’s entire value chain, according to a notice issued by Ministry of Commerce.
The ministry has designated 40 pilot cities to lead reforms in automotive distribution and retail consumption.
*Link (Chinese): https://t.co/IUITXeD1ub

➡ In a separate notice, China outlined measures to cultivate and scale the automotive aftermarket, specifically calling for the establishment of a robust regulatory framework for vehicle modification and the supply chain upgrading of the recreational vehicle manufacturing sector.
*Link (Chinese): https://t.co/q1WwQn7gt0

tweets.text:
*🚢 6/22/26 PM Hormuz crossing update⬆️* 
 
Hormuz vessel crossings are picking up steam again, back up to 24 over the prior 24 hours. Worth noting that if you're curious about the specific ships within the index, you can drill down to vessel type and even the individual vessel details for each chokepoint on @TheTerminal. Feel free to shoot me an IB.     

The index tracks every commercial crossing via AIS transponder data, refreshed every 30 minutes.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
*6/22 Hormuz Vessel Crossings — A Fragile Bounce Back* 

The initial post-MOU surge of 23 crossings on June 18th quickly evaporated over the weekend, bottoming out at just 7 transits on June 20th. Today's data shows a modest, fragile bounce back to 15 total commercial crossings https://t.co/cdHQc1BPnQ

Open source vs frontier model capabilities is another important factor determining where the value will accrue in the AI supply chain.

Below are the results of our own internal benchmarking.

Open source is getting better but there is still a meaningful gap from our perspective
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: Offering our Caliban research product means constantly testing the best AI models. We benchmarked leading open models against frontier closed models using real 3FR Caliban research staff requests. The gap has narrowed, but remains meaningful. We're sticking with GPT 5.5 for now https://t.co/QRdomP7ZWb

tweets.text:
"The magic of tokens that's not really viable for stocks is that you could keep minting on the other side... totally decoupled from the fees coming in."

@eddylazzarin on the buy-and-burn vs. mint-and-spend, and the lever token networks have that equity doesn't:

"The idea of the buy and burn is that you take revenue in one side, you charge whatever you can get away with in terms of your price. You take that revenue and use it to burn the token... reducing its supply, which helps it appreciate."

"The mirror of the buy and burn is the mint and spend. And this is where you are minting tokens out of nowhere to pay for whatever you need for growth or for network activities."

"You can keep pulling in fees and test your ability to charge in the market while completely separately scaling your spending in order to facilitate the most growth."

"In stocks, you can't pay an onboarding bonus to Uber drivers or whatever with your stock... To pay for growth, a conventional company has to do it in a very indirect way. They have to sell the equity to an investor in a private round to get cash to spend on growth. But this allows you to circumvent that completely."
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Crypto has been walled off from the real economy for years. That's changing. @eddylazzarin joins @smc90 and @rhackett to break down why crypto is entering a completely different phase, and what gets built once the rules finally catch up to the technology.  

00:00 Intro 
01:25 https://t.co/3A1xAVdzD1
tweets.full_text:
"The magic of tokens that's not really viable for stocks is that you could keep minting on the other side... totally decoupled from the fees coming in."

@eddylazzarin on the buy-and-burn vs. mint-and-spend, and the lever token networks have that equity doesn't:

"The idea of the buy and burn is that you take revenue in one side, you charge whatever you can get away with in terms of your price. You take that revenue and use it to burn the token... reducing its supply, which helps it appreciate."

"The mirror of the buy and burn is the mint and spend. And this is where you are minting tokens out of nowhere to pay for whatever you need for growth or for network activities."

"You can keep pulling in fees and test your ability to charge in the market while completely separately scaling your spending in order to facilitate the most growth."

"In stocks, you can't pay an onboarding bonus to Uber drivers or whatever with your stock... To pay for growth, a conventional company has to do it in a very indirect way. They have to sell the equity to an investor in a private round to get cash to spend on growth. But this allows you to circumvent that completely."

tweets.text:
Real yields continue to go vertical.

Now at two-year highs.

This is exactly how you break an economy drowning in debt.

The next Fed Chair has a lot of work to do, and that doesn't start with hiking rates.
